31.reason: [Incorrectly using scanf to read input during pop operation; should print the popped value instead of reading new input]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Incorrectly using scanf to read input during pop operation; should print the popped value instead of reading new input]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [In a pop operation, the program should output the top element and decrement the stack pointer, but instead it incorrectly tries to read input into the stack using scanf. This causes wrong behavior and potential undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [Incorrectly using scanf to read input during pop operation; should print the popped value instead of reading new input]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [In a pop operation, the program should output the top element, not read a new integer from input. This line incorrectly uses scanf instead of printf.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [Incorrectly using scanf to read input during a pop operation; should print the top element instead of reading into it]
